result.summary: Mapped 2 HTTP APIs for api.video: the main REST API at https://ws.api.video (Basic with project API key or bearer token) and the Admin API at https://admin.api.video (Basic with admin API key). No public GraphQL, MCP, or CLI surfaces found.

project_api_key.label: Project API key
project_api_key.setup: Sign up or log in at the [api.video dashboard](https://dashboard.api.video/overview). Open **API Keys** in the dashboard, choose the Sandbox or Production environment, and copy the project API key for that environment. The docs note that Sandbox and Production use separate keys, and Production can have multiple keys per application.

project_bearer_token.label: Disposable bearer access token
project_bearer_token.setup: First obtain a [project API key](https://dashboard.api.video/overview). Then exchange it for a short-lived bearer token by calling `POST https://ws.api.video/auth/api-key` (or the sandbox host) as described in the [Bearer token authentication guide](https://docs.api.video/reference/disposable-bearer-token-authentication). Refresh it with `POST https://ws.api.video/auth/refresh`. The returned `access_token` is valid for `3600` seconds.

admin_api_key.label: Admin API key
admin_api_key.setup: Request an admin API key from the [api.video customer support team](https://help.api.video/en/). The [Admin API overview](https://docs.api.video/reference/admin-api-overview) states this key is separate from per-project API keys and is required for `https://admin.api.video`.
